title: Semaglutide
about: Semaglutide is a derivative of the natural peptide GLP-1, which is recognized for its ability to lower blood sugar levels and enhance insulin secretion. Notably, research suggests that Semaglutide may also have beneficial effects on heart, liver, and lung function, while potentially slowing or preventing the effects of Alzheimer’s disease. Moreover, Semaglutide has been found to significantly reduce appetite by delaying gastric emptying and decreasing intestinal motility. This GLP-1 analog has demonstrated the capacity to stimulate insulin secretion and suppress glucagon secretion in a glucose-dependent manner.
description:
What Is BPC-157?
BPC-157, or Body Protection Compound-157, is derived from the naturally occurring protein, body protection compound (BPC), which is found in the human digestive tract. The primary function of BPC is to safeguard the gastrointestinal lining, promote healing, and facilitate blood vessel growth.

The synthetic version, BPC-157, is a pentadecapeptide composed of 15 amino acids, extracted from the larger BPC protein. Remarkably, it retains many of the curative properties observed in its parent molecule. Notably, BPC-157 exhibits beneficial effects in the following areas:

  • Wound healing
  • Promotion of blood vessel growth
  • Influence on the coagulation cascade
  • Generation of nitric oxide
  • Enhancement of immune system function
  • Modulation of gene expression
  • Regulation of hormones, particularly within the gastrointestinal nervous system.
BPC-157 Peptide Structure
Sequence: Gly-Glu-Pro-Pro-Pro-Gly-Lys-Pro-Ala-Asp-Asp-Ala-Gly-Leu-Val
Molecular Formula: C62H98N15O22
Molecular Weight: 1419.556 g/mol
PubChem CID: 108101
BPC-157 Peptide Research
1. BPC-157 and Wound Healing
The natural role of BPC in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract is to safeguard the integrity of the mucosal barrier, providing protection to underlying tissues against the harmful effects of gastric acid, bile, and other digestive compounds essential for nutrient absorption from food. A crucial aspect of this function involves the recruitment of fibroblasts. BPC-157 exhibits a dose-dependent impact on fibroblast behavior both in culture and in vivo, leading to enhanced proliferation and migration of these cells[1]. Fibroblasts play a vital role in the healing process as they are responsible for depositing essential extracellular matrix proteins such as collagen, fibrin, elastin, and others.
2. Vascular Growth and Collateralization
BPC-157 is a potent angiogenic factor known for significantly boosting the proliferation and growth of endothelial cells, which are responsible for lining blood vessels[1][2][3]. Studies conducted on rats have demonstrated that the peptide notably accelerates the growth of collateral blood vessels in the context of ischemia[4]. While this effect has been primarily observed in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract, there is growing evidence suggesting similar benefits in cardiovascular, neurological, and muscle tissues. This indicates that BPC-157 may hold promise as both a therapy for stroke and heart attack scenarios and as an investigational peptide to explore mechanisms for promoting healing after ischemic injuries[5][6].

Furthermore, research in chicken embryos suggests that BPC-157’s promotion of vascular growth is partly achieved through stimulating VEGFR2, a cell surface receptor that plays an active role in the nitric oxide signaling pathway[4][7][8]. VEGFR2 is believed to be vital for endothelial cell growth, proliferation, and longevity.
Cell culture studies have successfully shown that BPC-157 administration leads to the phenomenon of “vascular running.” Vascular running refers to the growth of blood vessels towards an injured or occluded area to restore blood flow to the surrounding tissue, safeguarding cellular function[9]. This unique capability of BPC-157 opens up the potential for developing an effective oral treatment for slowly developing arterial occlusions, commonly observed in conditions like atherosclerotic heart disease. In the future, this research area could potentially reduce the need for invasive surgical interventions such as stenting and coronary artery bypass grafting.
3. BPC-157 and Tendon Healing
Considering its role in fibroblast recruitment and blood vessel growth, it is not surprising that BPC-157 has demonstrated promising results in animal models of tendon, ligament, bone, and other connective tissue injuries. Tendon and ligament injuries tend to heal slowly, primarily due to limited blood supply in these tissues. The inadequate blood flow hinders the timely arrival of fibroblasts and other wound-healing cells to the injured area, leading to restricted overall repair. Both in vitro and in vivo studies involving rat tendons have revealed that BPC-157 enhances collateralization and increases fibroblast density in the context of tendon, ligament, and bone injuries. These findings suggest that BPC-157 is more effective in promoting healing in these tissues compared to bFGF, EGF, and VGF hormones[10].

Moreover, experiments employing FITC-phalloidin staining have demonstrated that BPC-157 acts as a potent stimulator of F-actin formation in fibroblasts[11]. F-actin is crucial for cell structure and function, particularly in cell migration. Analysis using western blotting has further indicated that BPC-157 enhances the phosphorylation of paxillin and FAK proteins, which are essential components of the cell migration pathway[12].

title: Tirzepatide
about: Tirzepatide is a synthetic derivative that combines the functionalities of gastric inhibitory polypeptide (GIP) and glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1). This unique combination enables Tirzepatide to effectively lower blood glucose levels, enhance insulin sensitivity, promote satiety, and facilitate weight loss. Initially developed to combat type 2 diabetes, Tirzepatide has demonstrated additional benefits in protecting the cardiovascular system and acting as a potent weight loss agent. Moreover, Semaglutide has been found to significantly reduce appetite by delaying gastric emptying and decreasing intestinal motility. This GLP-1 analog has demonstrated the capacity to stimulate insulin secretion and suppress glucagon secretion in a glucose-dependent manner.

title: BPC-157
about: BPC 157 is a penta-decapeptide comprising 15 amino acids, originally discovered and isolated from human gastric juice. Animal studies demonstrate its ability to accelerate healing in various wounds, including muscles, tendons, and ligaments. Moreover, BPC 157 offers organ protection and aids in preventing gastric ulcers. It acts systemically in the digestive tract to address leaky gut, IBS, gastro-intestinal cramps, and Crohn’s disease. Additionally, this peptide exhibits analgesic properties and enhances the healing of skin burns by increasing blood flow to damaged tissues. Its effects include speeding up reticulin and collagen formation, promoting angiogenesis, and stimulating macrophages and fibroblasts infiltration, making it a promising therapeutic tool for wound healing management.

title: CJC-1295
about: CJC-1295 is a synthetic analogue of growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) designed to boost growth hormone and insulin-like growth factor 1 (IGF-1) levels in the plasma. The addition of DAC extends the plasma half-life of CJC-1295.

title: Ipamorelin
about: Ipamorelin is a pentapeptide, consisting of five amino acids in its structure. Functioning as a GH secretagogue, it acts as an agonist by binding to specific cell receptors and eliciting a cellular response. In animal test subjects, Ipamorelin stimulates the pituitary gland, leading to increased production of growth-related secretions. Simultaneously, it inhibits the secretion of somatostatin, a hormone that regulates growth. Moreover, Ipamorelin has been found to enhance the production of IGF-1, or Insulin-like Growth Factor 1, which plays a crucial role in the growth and repair of muscular and skeletal tissue.

title: Sermorelin
about: Sermorelin is a growth-hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) analogue used clinically to assess growth hormone secretion. It is of interest to researchers for its ability to improve bone density, reduce scaring, fight the effects of dementia, and reduce seizure activity.

title: Kisspeptin-10
about: Kisspeptin acts to control hormone secretion as it relates to reproduction. Kisspeptin can influence testosterone levels and sex-related behaviors like drive and motivation. Research also suggests it may help to reverse some of the effects of aging.

title: AOD9604
about: AOD9604 is a derivative of human growth hormone (hGH) and a modified version of the hGH fragment 176-191 peptide, incorporating a di-sulfide bridge. Initially designed as a lipolytic (fat-burning) compound, AOD9604 has displayed promising results in research related to heart disease, osteoarthritis/cartilage repair, and metabolic syndrome. In animal studies, AOD9604 has demonstrated the ability to stimulate lipolysis, which is the breakdown or destruction of fat, while also inhibiting lipogenesis.

title: Gonadorelin
about: Gonadorelin, a gonadotropin-releasing hormone agonist, has demonstrated significant benefits in treating infertility and hypogonadism. Recent research indicates that it might hold potential in stopping the growth of breast and prostate cancer. Furthermore, there are promising findings suggesting its efficacy in the treatment of Alzheimer’s disease.

title: TB-500
about: Thymosin Beta-4 consists of a 43 amino acid peptide sequence. Animal studies have revealed its beneficial effects, including: promoting blood vessel growth, regulating wound healing, reducing inflammation, and mitigating oxidative damage in both the heart and central nervous system. Thymosin Beta-4 plays a vital role in safeguarding tissues, supporting tissue repair, regeneration, and remodeling in cases of injury or damage. Furthermore, it has garnered significant attention in anti-aging research.
